expect: Retired periodics stop resurfacing as zombie DUE tasks, and task_order aligns with maintenance periodics after rerun.
actual: CONFIRMED. task_order_helpers.get_done_periodic_ids() now treats retired periodics as resolved, so zombie filtering no longer re-surfaces retired periodic IDs as DUE work. Regression added in tools/test_task_order_race.py. Live rerun of task_order.py dropped the false DUE set ([periodics-meta-audit], [historian-routing], [external-scanning], [enforce-l-1212], [enforce-l-757], [enforce-l-835]) and aligned the ranked backlog with maintenance.py --quick. The audit also exposed one registry-note contradiction in tools/periodics.json: enforcement-audit's S533 note proposed cadence 3→10, but live hardening re-applies the N≥1000 FM-33 / L-1066 floor and keeps cadence_sessions at 3, so only the metadata note needed reconciliation.
diff: Expected retired periodics to disappear from task_order after the parser fix, and they did. Root cause was narrower than the stale state suggested: maintenance_signals.check_periodics() already skipped retired entries; the false backlog came from task_order's zombie filter not counting retired periodics as done. Secondary finding was metadata drift, not a surviving live cadence change: the S533 enforcement-relaxation note outlived the hardening rule that still clamps the registry to cadence 3.
